traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Creates more with size, fluidity, and mismatch reads than raw burst.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Usually keeps the possession moving and does not over-force counters.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can connect advantages from the wing-forward slot.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Jumper projects as an important swing piece of the profile.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Size and movement make him easy to fit around stronger creators.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Reads are generally on time for a young hybrid forward.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Should scale because the profile does not demand offensive ownership.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Can credibly defend across forward matchups and survive switching.

summary.synopsis: Long-term hybrid forward bet built on size, connectivity, and two-way lineup fit more than immediate offensive dominance.
strengths: Scalable forward frame with real defensive utility.
Plays a connector style that should travel into better lineups.
Jumper path is good enough to keep real upside on the table.
weaknesses: Does not yet create easy offense on his own.
Still needs more physical certainty to handle tougher interior assignments.
swingFactors: Whether the jumper becomes a real spacing lever.
How much on-ball scoring pressure he can actually absorb.

projection.bestOutcome: High-end two-way forward connector who closes games because the fit is clean on both ends.
projection.medianOutcome: Starting combo forward with scalable defense and enough offensive feel to stay additive.
projection.riskSummary: The role floor is real, but the ceiling still depends on offensive force becoming more than connective value.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Can turn shooting threat into real second-side creation.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Still learning how much creation burden he can handle efficiently.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Passing is functional but not a driver.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Shotmaking is the defining offensive force in the profile.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Movement shooting and spacing value should travel easily.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Offensive reads are improving but not yet surgical.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Easy offensive fit if the defense holds enough weight.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Defensive outcomes still depend on added strength and consistency.

summary.synopsis: Shotmaking wing whose best pathway is as a spacing-and-scoring pressure source rather than a do-everything creator.
strengths: Real jumper gravity changes coverage behavior.
Can score without monopolizing the offense.
Size is good enough for wing lineup structures.
weaknesses: Defensive floor still needs work.
Playmaking does not yet lift the rest of the offense.
swingFactors: Whether the body and defense stabilize enough for playoff lineups.
How much self-creation he adds beyond shotmaking.

projection.bestOutcome: Dynamic scoring wing who tilts spacing and gives lineups a real shotmaking release valve.
projection.medianOutcome: Rotation scoring wing whose value is tied to offense-first lineup construction.
projection.riskSummary: The jumper is bankable enough to keep a role alive, but the long-term value depends on defensive viability.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Can attack tilted defenders but is not a primary engine.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Generally keeps the ball moving within a secondary role.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Passing is more connective than inventive.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Shooting remains the cleanest offensive translation point.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Should fit lineups that already have creators.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Reads are fine for a role wing, not yet standout.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Simple offensive role makes the fit straightforward.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Defense still projects more adequate than value-adding.

summary.synopsis: Shooting-oriented wing whose clearest value is as a low-maintenance offensive complement.
strengths: Jumper gives him immediate role clarity.
Does not need many touches to contribute.
Wing size keeps viable lineup combinations open.
weaknesses: Does not bring much primary creation.
Defensive ceiling looks more neutral than additive.
swingFactors: Whether the defense clears a trustworthy playoff threshold.
How much secondary closeout creation becomes available.

projection.bestOutcome: Reliable rotation wing whose shooting and size make him easy to keep on the floor.
projection.medianOutcome: Bench scoring wing who fits lineups needing spacing more than creation.
projection.riskSummary: He has a clean offensive lane, but the overall value depends on whether the defense holds enough weight.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Offense still comes from touch, positioning, and simple counters.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Usually makes the right simple frontcourt read.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Passing is more connective than creation-based.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Any floor-spacing value is still developmental.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Activity, screening, and finishing should translate.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Defensive reads are encouraging for a young big.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Projects as a useful piece if the defense continues to travel.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: The defense is the real selling point in the profile.

summary.synopsis: Young frontcourt defender with enough feel and activity to project into modern rotation big minutes.
strengths: Defensive processing looks starter-viable.
Off-ball big tools translate without special play calls.
Plays within structure and does not force offense.
weaknesses: Offensive ceiling remains limited.
Spacing value is still mostly projective.
swingFactors: Whether the jumper becomes usable enough to keep lineups open.
How much physical upside arrives without flattening mobility.

projection.bestOutcome: Two-way rotation big who survives high-level defensive scheme demands and stays playable offensively.
projection.medianOutcome: Low-maintenance rotation big built around defense and functional feel.
projection.riskSummary: The defensive base gives him a path, but the offensive value still needs a real separator.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Can generate offense with pace changes and scoring aggression.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Still toggles between advantage creation and overdribble risk.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Passing is useful, though not the driving value prop.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Jumper matters enough to keep defenders honest.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Best with the offense flowing through him or near him.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Processing still needs to become cleaner possession to possession.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Scales best if the shot and decisions settle at a higher level.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Defense remains a role-management question.

summary.synopsis: Aggressive scoring guard whose pathway is built on shotmaking and advantage pressure rather than floor-general control.
strengths: Can create tough offense from the perimeter.
Has enough shotmaking to keep a real offensive lane alive.
Brings scoring confidence that can swing bench units.
weaknesses: Decision quality still lags the scoring ambition.
Defensive value is not yet helping the profile.
swingFactors: Whether the passing sharpens enough for bigger offensive trust.
How much defensive survivability he can build.

projection.bestOutcome: Microwave plus guard with enough creation and shotmaking to close offense-first games.
projection.medianOutcome: Bench scoring guard whose role depends on streak scoring and lineup insulation.
projection.riskSummary: The scoring talent is real, but the profile is fragile unless the possessions become cleaner.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Can get into shots and lean on scoring talent quickly.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Still more scorer-brained than orchestrator-brained.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Passing is a secondary tool for now.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Shotmaking pressure is the cleanest offensive value.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Can still matter as a spacing and movement weapon.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Reads are not yet consistently fast enough for full offensive trust.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Scales if the defense and decisions settle.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Defense is still a question mark rather than a positive.

summary.synopsis: Scoring wing prospect with real shotmaking pop and a wider-than-usual range of offensive outcomes.
strengths: Can create and make difficult perimeter shots.
Shot gravity gives him a natural lane into lineups.
Wing size keeps higher-end outcomes alive.
weaknesses: Defensive value is still speculative.
Playmaking and decision quality remain behind the scoring tools.
swingFactors: Whether the on-ball process catches up to the shotmaking.
How much defensive discipline he can add.

projection.bestOutcome: Dynamic scoring wing whose shooting pressure lets him function as a featured option.
projection.medianOutcome: Shotmaking wing scorer who fits best in offense-first lineup constructions.
projection.riskSummary: The offensive talent gives him a real ceiling, but the role range stays wide until the rest of the game sharpens.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Has some mismatch scoring tools but not yet reliable separation.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Still learning how much to force against pro-level size and pace.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Passing remains a secondary developmental skill.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Jumper is important to the profile and gives him a clean pathway.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Should fit off stronger creators if the shot keeps moving forward.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Processing still looks young against professional context.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Forward size keeps fit paths available if the offense stabilizes.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Can survive multiple forward looks, but needs more consistency.

summary.synopsis: Young international forward bet built more on long-term offensive upside than immediate finished utility.
strengths: Forward size plus skill gives him a real developmental runway.
Jumper keeps a clear role pathway available.
Already operating in a professional context.
weaknesses: Still raw in processing and decision pace.
Not yet physically or skillfully sturdy enough for a high-floor projection.
swingFactors: Whether the shooting becomes a true bankable weapon.
How much creation and defensive consistency arrive over time.

projection.bestOutcome: Scoring forward with enough size and shotmaking to become a real offensive chess piece.
projection.medianOutcome: Developmental rotation forward whose value depends on spacing and growth.
projection.riskSummary: The age and tools are attractive, but the profile still requires a meaningful amount of projection.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Mostly operates as a play finisher and simple counter player.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: Plays within structure and avoids ugly possessions.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can connect actions without functioning as a hub.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Shot is still more hope than pressure source.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Rebounding and physical utility give him role clarity.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Defensive reads are solid enough for a role big.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Low-maintenance style should translate into rotation utility.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Defense is the cleaner path to staying on the floor.

summary.synopsis: Functional frontcourt role player whose best case is built on defense, rebounding, and connective play.
strengths: Does the simple frontcourt work well.
Defensive value is easier to trust than offensive upside.
Should fit lineups that need utility instead of touches.
weaknesses: Offensive ceiling is limited.
Does not bring a clear spacing lever yet.
swingFactors: Whether the jumper becomes enough to keep the paint cleaner.
How much defensive versatility he can sustain at higher levels.

projection.bestOutcome: Reliable rotation big-forward who stays playable because the role utility is real.
projection.medianOutcome: Depth frontcourt piece built on rebounding and defense.
projection.riskSummary: The role is understandable, but the offensive value needs something more than pure utility.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Creates enough through feel and quick decisions, not pressure scoring.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: One of the cleaner decision-makers in this range.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Can keep offense connected from the forward slot.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Useful enough spacing to keep him viable in modern lineups.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Knows how to fit next to stronger offensive teammates.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: Processes quickly on both sides of the ball.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Scales because he complements rather than competes with primary talent.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Good enough defensively to survive across forward looks.

summary.synopsis: Winning forward connector who projects more through feel and lineup fit than raw upside.
strengths: Makes fast, useful decisions.
Fits around stars without needing usage.
Brings enough two-way competence to survive different lineup shapes.
weaknesses: Does not create much individual offense.
Ceiling is limited because the profile is role-oriented.
swingFactors: Whether the shooting becomes a more respected lever.
How much defensive range he can hold against NBA athletes.

projection.bestOutcome: Highly useful connector forward who plays in a lot of winning lineups.
projection.medianOutcome: Reliable rotation forward built on feel, spacing, and quick decisions.
projection.riskSummary: The role floor is fairly stable, but the upside is capped unless the shot becomes more threatening.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Creates enough advantages through craft and pace.

traits.name: Decision Making
traits.note: One of the cleaner possession managers in this tier.

traits.name: Passing Creation
traits.note: Consistently finds useful passes without overcomplicating reads.

traits.name: Shooting Gravity
traits.note: Jumper keeps defenders honest and supports the control game.

traits.name: Off-Ball Value
traits.note: Best when the offense runs through him to some degree.

traits.name: Processing Speed
traits.note: High processing floor is the core reason to buy the profile.

traits.name: Scalability
traits.note: Works best in skill-first lineups that can cover some physical gaps.

traits.name: Defensive Versatility
traits.note: Defense is the clearer limiter on the role size.

summary.synopsis: Older lead-guard prospect whose value is built on control, passing, and low-mistake orchestration.
strengths: Processes quickly and keeps the offense steady.
Passing is a real NBA-caliber trait in this tier.
Can run possessions without chaos.
weaknesses: Does not bring major size or defensive margin for error.
Athletic ceiling caps the top-end creation projection.
swingFactors: Whether the shot stays strong enough for better defenders.
How well he survives being targeted defensively.

projection.bestOutcome: Steady rotation lead guard who keeps good offenses on schedule.
projection.medianOutcome: Backup or bridge guard whose value comes from control and passing.
projection.riskSummary: The floor is supported by feel, but the role size is constrained by physical limitations.

traits.name: Advantage Creation
traits.note: Still more developmental than reliable with the ball.

traits.note: Shows connective instincts even when the skill level is still forming.

summary.synopsis: Young guard-wing bet whose long-term value starts with defense, size, and connective feel rather than finished offense.

weaknesses: Jumper is still the swing question.

How much on-ball comfort arrives without losing the defensive identity.

projection.bestOutcome: Two-way big guard who can defend up a slot and keep offense moving.
projection.medianOutcome: Developmental rotation guard-wing whose role is shaped by defense and connective play.

traits.name: Advantage Creation

summary.synopsis: Young defensive big swing whose draft value is driven overwhelmingly by the chance to become a frontcourt problem-solver.

summary.synopsis: Strong hybrid forward bet built around physical utility, connective feel, and a broad enough two-way pathway.

summary.synopsis: Young lead-guard swing whose value depends on live-dribble creation maturing into real offensive control.
